title: lappula rupicola
intro: Lappula rupicola, also known as the alpine bugloss, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae. Traditionally, Lappula rupicola has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to treat digestive problems. Lappula rupicola can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, with its leaves and flowers being used in various herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
